**Cuneo** (Italian: [[ˈkuːneo]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:It-Cuneo.ogg "File:It-Cuneo.ogg"); [Piedmontese](/wiki/Piedmontese_language "Piedmontese language"): *Coni* [[ˈkʊni]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Piedmontese "Help:IPA/Piedmontese"); [Occitan](/wiki/Occitan_language "Occitan language"): *Coni* [[ˈkuni]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Occitan "Help:IPA/Occitan"); [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Coni* [[kɔni]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a city and *[comune](/wiki/Comune "Comune")* in [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), the capital of the [province of Cuneo](/wiki/Province_of_Cuneo "Province of Cuneo"), the fourth largest of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy")'s provinces by area.

It is located at an elevation of 550 metres (1,800 ft) in the south-west of [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont"), at the confluence of the rivers [Stura](/wiki/Stura_di_Demonte "Stura di Demonte") and [Gesso](/w/index.php?title=Gesso_(river)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gesso (river) (page does not exist)").

Cuneo is bounded by the municipalities of [Beinette](/wiki/Beinette "Beinette"), [Borgo San Dalmazzo](/wiki/Borgo_San_Dalmazzo "Borgo San Dalmazzo"), [Boves](/wiki/Boves,_Piedmont "Boves, Piedmont"), [Busca](/wiki/Busca,_Piedmont "Busca, Piedmont"), [Caraglio](/wiki/Caraglio "Caraglio"), [Castelletto Stura](/wiki/Castelletto_Stura "Castelletto Stura"), [Centallo](/wiki/Centallo "Centallo"), [Cervasca](/wiki/Cervasca "Cervasca"), [Morozzo](/wiki/Morozzo "Morozzo"), [Peveragno](/wiki/Peveragno "Peveragno"), [Tarantasca](/wiki/Tarantasca "Tarantasca") and [Vignolo](/wiki/Vignolo "Vignolo").[[3]](#cite_note-inhabitants_Cuneo-3)

It is located near six mountain passes:

* [Colle della Maddalena](/wiki/Colle_della_Maddalena "Colle della Maddalena") at 1,996 metres (6,549 ft)
* [Colle di Tenda](/wiki/Colle_di_Tenda "Colle di Tenda") at 1,871 metres (6,138 ft) – [Tunnel of Tenda](/w/index.php?title=Traforo_stradale_del_Colle_di_Tenda&action=edit&redlink=1 "Traforo stradale del Colle di Tenda (page does not exist)") at 1,300 metres (4,300 ft), 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) long
* [Colle del Melogno](/wiki/Colle_del_Melogno "Colle del Melogno") at 1,027 metres (3,369 ft)
* [Colle San Bernardo](/wiki/Colle_San_Bernardo "Colle San Bernardo") at 957 metres (3,140 ft)
* [Colle di Nava](/wiki/Colle_di_Nava "Colle di Nava") at 934 metres (3,064 ft)
* [Colle di Cadibona](/wiki/Bocchetta_di_Altare "Bocchetta di Altare") at 459 metres (1,506 ft).

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/eb/Cuneo_%E2%80%93_Via_Nizza_%28xilografia%29.jpg/250px-Cuneo_%E2%80%93_Via_Nizza_%28xilografia%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cuneo_%E2%80%93_Via_Nizza_(xilografia).jpg)

19th-century image of Cuneo

Cuneo was founded in 1198 by the local population, who declared it an independent commune, freeing themselves from the authority of the bishops of [Asti](/wiki/Asti "Asti") and the [marquisses of Montferrat](/wiki/Marquisate_of_Monferrat "Marquisate of Monferrat") and [Saluzzo](/wiki/Marquisate_of_Saluzzo "Marquisate of Saluzzo"). In 1210, the latter occupied it, and in 1231 the *Cuneesi* rebelled. In 1238, they were recognized as a free commune by [Emperor Frederick II](/wiki/Frederick_II,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Frederick II, Holy Roman Emperor").

In 1259, the independence of Cuneo ceased forever, as it gave itself, also to take protection against its more powerful neighbours, to [Charles I of Anjou](/wiki/Charles_I_of_Anjou "Charles I of Anjou"), who was then the [Count of Provence](/wiki/Count_of_Provence "Count of Provence"). Together with [Alba](/wiki/Alba,_Italy "Alba, Italy"), it was the main Angevine possession in Northern Italy; Angevine rule interrupted by periods under the control of Saluzzo, Savoy, and the [Visconti of Milan](/wiki/Visconti_of_Milan "Visconti of Milan") was ended in 1382 when Cuneo was acquired by the [Duchy of Savoy](/wiki/Duchy_of_Savoy "Duchy of Savoy").

Cuneo became an important stronghold of the expanding Savoy state. The city was thus besieged several times by France: first in 1515 by Swiss troops of [Francis I of France](/wiki/Francis_I_of_France "Francis I of France"), then again in 1542, 1557, 1639, 1641, 1691 and, during the [War of the Austrian Succession](/wiki/War_of_the_Austrian_Succession "War of the Austrian Succession"), in 1741. Cuneo resisted each siege successfully. The city was taken by [France](/wiki/First_French_Empire "First French Empire") only during the [Napoleonic Wars](/wiki/Napoleonic_Wars "Napoleonic Wars") and was made the capital of the [Stura](/wiki/Stura "Stura") department. After the restoration of the [Kingdom of Sardinia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sardinia "Kingdom of Sardinia"), and the [unification of Italy](/wiki/Unification_of_Italy "Unification of Italy"), Cuneo became the capital of its namesake province in 1859. In 1862, Cuneo was the location of a Polish Military School moved from [Genoa](/wiki/Genoa "Genoa"), which trained Polish officers in exile, the overwhelming majority of whom then fought in the Polish [January Uprising](/wiki/January_Uprising "January Uprising") in the [Russian Partition](/wiki/Russian_Partition "Russian Partition") of Poland in 1863–1864 (see also *[Italy–Poland relations](/wiki/Italy%E2%80%93Poland_relations "Italy–Poland relations")*).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

During [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), from 1943 to 1945, it was one of the main centres of [partisan resistance](/wiki/Italian_resistance_movement "Italian resistance movement") against the German occupation of Italy.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
In 1943, Cuneo's Jewish citizens were briefly arrested and imprisoned at the nearby [Borgo San Dalmazzo concentration camp](/wiki/Borgo_San_Dalmazzo_concentration_camp "Borgo San Dalmazzo concentration camp") by the order of Minister of the Interior [Guido Buffarini Guidi](/wiki/Guido_Buffarini_Guidi "Guido Buffarini Guidi"). They were freed before the Minister's orders came into effect and most community members fled Cuneo into hiding.

However, on 9 December 1944, the Cuneo Police Department reopened the camp and imprisoned the remaining Jewish residents of Cuneo most of whom were then deported to [Auschwitz](/wiki/Auschwitz "Auschwitz"). Few survived according to reports. Italian partisans liberated Cuneo from the German and Italian fascist occupation on 25 April 1945. The retreating fascist forces murdered the remaining six Jewish prisoners being held at Cuneo's local prison.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Climate")]

Cuneo has a temperate sub-continental climate, with cold winters and hot, dry summers. However, it is situated more than 500 metres (1,640 feet) [above sea level](/wiki/Above_mean_sea_level "Above mean sea level"), which helps to make summers more bearable: the hottest month, July, has an average temperature of 21.6 °C (70.9 °F). The coldest, January, averages 1.7 °C (35.1 °F). Annual precipitation is about 962 mm (37.9 in), distributed over 81 days. The rainfall pattern is similar to that of Turin, with two maxima—one primary and one secondary (spring and autumn) and two minima (summer and winter). The driest month is July, 44 millimetres (1.7 in). Snowfalls are frequent owing to high elevation and wind patterns.

## Cuisine and food

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Cuisine and food")]

Cuneo's specialty is *[Cuneesi al rhum](/w/index.php?title=Cuneesi_al_rhum&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cuneesi al rhum (page does not exist)")*, small meringues with dark chocolate coating and a rum-based chocolate filling. They are a creation of Andrea Arione (1923), who also registered the name, and sold them in the bar still located in the central square, Piazza Galimberti; another claim makes them a creation of pastry chef Pietro Galletti from [Dronero](/wiki/Dronero "Dronero"). Another specialty is "raviolini al plin", a small ravioli pasta made with meat and vegetables.

## Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sport")]

### Volleyball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Volleyball")]

There is an important volleyball club, [Piemonte Volley](/wiki/Piemonte_Volley "Piemonte Volley") who won 1 [Italian Volleyball League](/wiki/Italian_Volleyball_League "Italian Volleyball League"), 3 [CEV Cup](/wiki/CEV_Cup "CEV Cup"), 2 CEV SuperCup, 4 Italian Volleyball Cup and 3 Italian Volleyball SuperCup.

### Football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Football")]

Associazione Calcio Cuneo 1905 ([A.C. Cuneo 1905](/wiki/A.C._Cuneo_1905 "A.C. Cuneo 1905")) who plays in the 3rd level of Italian football.

### Rugby

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Rugby")]

Cuneo has also a Rugby Team called "Cuneo Pedona Rugby", currently playing in the National "Serie C" League. The team is playing in the Municipal Field of Madonna dell'Olmo.

### Cycling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Cycling")]

Many times stage of [Giro d'Italia](/wiki/Giro_d%27Italia "Giro d'Italia"). In 2016, for the first time in the Giro history, the race arrived in Sant'Anna di Vinadio sanctuary, the highest sanctuary in Europe, 2035 m, and the day after, on 29 May, the race started from Cuneo.

Since 1987 Cuneo has been the start and arrival point of the amateur international race "La Fausto Coppi".

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cuneo&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Transport")]

The city is served by [Cuneo International Airport](/wiki/Cuneo_International_Airport "Cuneo International Airport") which is located 20 km (12 mi) north of the city centre. However, the airport only provides routes to limited destinations, the nearest domestic and international airport is [Turin Airport](/wiki/Turin_Airport "Turin Airport"), located 129 km (80 mi) north east of Cuneo. Other nearby airports can also be received such as [Nice Côte d'Azur Airport](/wiki/Nice_C%C3%B4te_d%27Azur_Airport "Nice Côte d'Azur Airport") in France, located 136 km (85 mi) south west and [Genoa Cristoforo Colombo Airport](/wiki/Genoa_Cristoforo_Colombo_Airport "Genoa Cristoforo Colombo Airport"), located 142 km (88 mi) west of Cuneo.
